title: aquilaria rugosa
intro: Aquilaria rugosa, also known as the Malabar agalloch or Malabar aloeswood, is a species of tree in the Thymelaeaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The resinous heartwood of this tree has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Aquilaria rugosa have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting against infections, and even exhibiting anticancer properties. The resinous heartwood is often used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and extracts, which are believed to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
